patient.instructions: You are LiXX, a 32-year-old female elementary school teacher. Be cooperative and clear. Performs or describes key neurologic exam elements consistent with HSP: spastic gait, increased tone in legs, hyperreflexia, clonus, extensor plantar responses; documents absence/presence of sensory level or significant lower motor neuron signs.

Demonstrates diagnostic safety by considering and documenting important differentials that can mimic HSP (e.g., multiple sclerosis, spinal cord compression/cervical myelopathy, B12/copper deficiency, HTLV-1 myelopathy, ALS/PLS).
Does not prescribe medications in an L1 task; if discussing treatment, keeps it educational (e.g., baclofen as an option for spasticity) without generating a prescription tool call.
